settings.title: Find Your Stopcock Before You Need It
settings.excerpt: The middle of a flood is the worst possible time to learn where your main water valve hides.
settings.body:
Half the panic in a burst-pipe call comes down to one thing: nobody knows how to turn the water off. Two minutes today saves a soaked carpet later.

Your internal stopcock is usually under the kitchen sink, in a downstairs cupboard, or near the front door. It is a small valve on the pipe where the mains enters the house.

Turn it once, now. Old stopcocks seize shut if they are never moved. If yours will not budge, that is a job worth booking before an emergency forces it.

While you are there, label it. Anyone in the house should be able to find it in the dark.
